fields.title: Network Infrastructure Design Lab
fields.description: Trainees design fibre optic network layouts and calculate link budgets.

fields.title: Hands-on Fibre Optic Splicing Lab
fields.description: Trainees gain practical experience in fusion splicing, mechanical splicing, and connector termination.

fields.title: Certification Preparation Programme
fields.description: Structured preparation sessions for industry-recognised certifications in fibre optics.

fields.title: Safety and Compliance Training
fields.description: Essential training in workplace safety, PPE usage, and regulatory compliance.

fields.title: OTDR and Power Meter Practical Sessions
fields.description: Trainees learn to operate optical time-domain reflectometers and power meters for fibre characterisation.

fields.title: Portfolio Development Workshop
fields.description: Trainees build professional portfolios showcasing their technical projects and certifications.

fields.title: Field Demonstrations at Active Deployment Sites
fields.description: Trainees visit live fibre deployment projects to observe cable blowing and aerial installation.

fields.title: Industry Masterclasses with Leading Engineers
fields.description: Senior engineers deliver intensive sessions on advanced topics in network infrastructure.

fields.title: Broadband Deployment and Last-Mile Connectivity
fields.description: Focused training on broadband network deployment strategies for underserved areas.

fields.title: Community Outreach: Digital Literacy Workshop in Kiambu County
fields.content: Trainees from the Inuka Digital Leap programme led a digital literacy workshop for local youth in Kiambu County. The workshop covered topics such as basic internet navigation, online safety, and career pathways in technology.

fields.title: Kenya Pipeline Foundation Commits to Digital Skills Development
fields.content: The Kenya Pipeline Foundation has announced a significant commitment to expanding the Inuka Digital Leap programme, pledging resources to scale the initiative to reach more young Kenyans across additional counties. This partnership will focus on addressing the critical skills gap in fibre optics engineering, a field experiencing unprecedented demand as Kenya accelerates its national fibre rollout.

fields.title: Why Fibre Optics is the Next Frontier for Kenya's Youth
fields.content: Kenya's digital transformation is being built on a foundation of fibre optics. As the government pushes forward with its ambitious national fibre rollout, the demand for skilled fibre optic technicians has skyrocketed. The Inuka Digital Leap programme is helping to fill this gap by providing practical, industry-aligned training in fibre optic engineering.

fields.title: JKUAT and JHUB Africa Sign MoU to Expand Digital Skills Programme
fields.content: Jomo Kenyatta University of Agriculture and Technology and JHUB Africa have signed a renewed Memorandum of Understanding that expands the Inuka Digital Leap programme to four additional counties. The expanded partnership will establish new training centres in Kisumu, Nakuru, Meru, and Mombasa.

fields.title: Cohort 1 Trainees Begin Industry Attachments Across Kenya
fields.content: The trainees of Cohort 1 have commenced their industry attachments with partner organisations across Kenya, marking the final phase of the Inuka Digital Leap programme. Each trainee has been assigned to a host organisation that aligns with their area of specialisation.

fields.title: Industry Mentors Share Insights on Kenya's Digital Infrastructure Future
fields.content: The Inuka Digital Leap programme hosted a mentorship forum featuring senior engineers and technology leaders from Kenya's leading telecommunications companies including Safaricom, Telkom Kenya, and several fibre network operators.

fields.title: Women in Tech: Breaking Barriers in Network Infrastructure
fields.content: With 42% female representation in its first cohort, the Inuka Digital Leap programme is demonstrating that gender inclusion is not just a goal but an achievable reality. The trainees are thriving in what has historically been a male-dominated technical field.

fields.title: Inuka Digital Leap First Cohort of 15 Trailblazers
fields.content: The Inuka Digital Leap Programme marked a defining milestone with the formation of its inaugural cohort of 15 trainees, who successfully applied, were selected, and formally enrolled into the intensive training pathway on June 15, 2026. The cohort represents the first intake under the programme structured digital workforce development framework. The launch and recognition event, hosted at the Jomo Kenyatta University of Agriculture and Technology (JKUAT) Main Campus, brought together stakeholders from industry, government, and the families of the trainees to acknowledge the significance of this pioneering group. Over a six-month period, Cohort 1 underwent rigorous, competency-based training covering network infrastructure, fibre optic engineering, broadband deployment, and cloud engineering.

fields.title: Trainees Complete First Round of Fibre Optic Splice Certification
fields.content: All 15 members of Cohort 1 have successfully passed their Fibre Optic Splice Certification examination. The practical examination, administered by certified industry assessors, tested the trainees proficiency in fusion splicing, mechanical splicing, and connector termination. The pass rate of 100% reflects both the dedication of the trainees and the effectiveness of the programme hands-on training approach.

fields.title: Field Visit: Trainees Experience Real-World Fibre Deployment
fields.content: The trainees of Cohort 1 stepped out of the classroom and into the field for an immersive day of learning at active fibre deployment sites across Nairobi. The field visit gave trainees a firsthand look at real-world network deployment operations.

fields.description: The Kenya Pipeline Company Foundation champions corporate social responsibility by funding digital skills development programmes that create sustainable economic opportunities for Kenyan youth.
